title: Chottanikkara Temple
tagline: Chottanikkara Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Chottanikkara Bhagavathy Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Bhagavathy, located in the village of Chottanikkara within the Ernakulam District of Kerala, India.
overview.description: The Chottanikkara Bhagavathy Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Bhagavathy, located in the village of Chottanikkara within the Ernakulam District of Kerala, India. Managed by the Cochin Devaswom Board, it is one of the most significant shrines among the 409 temples under the board's administration. The temple's central deity is the Divine Mother, worshipped as Rajarajeswari (Adiparashakthi) in three distinct forms throughout the day: Saraswathi in the morning, Lakshmi at noon, and Durga in the evening. An idol of Mahavishnu shares the same pedestal, leading to the collective nomencl

topPlaces.name: Chottanikkara
topPlaces.description: Chottanikkara is a temple town and a southern suburb of the city of Kochi, Kerala, India. In local administration, it is a panchayat which consists of Chottanikkara, Kanayannur, Eruvely, Kureekkad, Vattukkunnu, Palace Square and Kottayathupara villages.

topPlaces.name: Mamala
topPlaces.description: Mamala is a village near Thiruvankulam in the state of Kerala, India. It is situated around 9 km (5.6 mi) from Vytilla Junction and around 13 km (8 mi) from Ernakulam South railway station.

topPlaces.name: St. John's Church, Thiruvaniyoor
topPlaces.description: St. John's Orthodox Syrian Church, also known as Kanniattunirappu Palli is a church part of the Kandanad West Diocese of Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church .

topPlaces.name: Thiruvankulam
topPlaces.description: Thiruvankulam is a census town in Thrippunithura municipality, in Ernakulam district, Kerala, India. The area is a part of the Kochi metropolitan area.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Kandanad
topPlaces.description: Kandanad Valiyapally St. Mary's Orthodox Cathedral, Kandanad is a cathedral in the Kandanad village of Ernakulam District of Kerala.

image.alt: St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Kandanad

topPlaces.name: Thiruvaniyoor
topPlaces.description: Thiruvaniyoor is an eastern suburb of the city of Kochi in the state of Kerala, India. Situated around 15 km (9 mi) south-east from the city centre, it is a part of Kunnathunad taluk.

topPlaces.name: Kureekkad
topPlaces.description: Kureekkad is a census town in Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is the gateway to two of the many suburbs of Kochi, Chottanikkara and Kandanad.

topPlaces.name: Udayamperoor Ekadasi Perumthrikovil Temple
topPlaces.description: Udayamperoor Ekadasi Perumthrikovil Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva at Udayamperoor of Ernakulam District in Kerala state in India. This temple is a classic example of the architectural style of Kerala and has monumental towers and Round Sanctum Sanctorum.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chottanikkara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chottanikkara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chottanikkara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (24 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (12 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chottanikkara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chottanikkara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chottanikkara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Chottanikkara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chottanikkara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chottanikkara, Mamala, St. John's Church, Thiruvaniyoor, Thiruvankulam, St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Kandanad.
